steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to provide an example of a dinosaur fossil specimen's nickname — the answer must name a specific nickname, not merely note that nicknames exist.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Nicknames are given to many dinosaur fossil specimens, including one housed at a museum in Denver, Colorado.: mentions a nicknamed specimen but never names the nickname. ✗
  • Choice B — Dinosaur fossil specimens can be found at museums all over the world, and many of these specimens are given nicknames.: only restates that nicknames exist — no specific nickname given. ✗
  • Choice C — Wyrex is the nickname of a Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen housed at the Denver Museum of Nature and Science in Denver, Colorado.: directly names the nickname 'Wyrex' and identifies the specimen, providing a concrete example. ✓
  • Choice D — A Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen from the Late Cretaceous period, which ended more than 65 million years ago, is housed at the Denver Museum of Nature and Science.: describes the specimen but omits its nickname entirely. ✗
That is the correct answer.
